blocks.body: Present Continuous: Forms and Use — am/is/are + verb-ing for actions happening now or temporarily.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Present Continuous, we use am / is / are + verb-ing for actions happening now or temporarily.","rules":[{"label":"am / is / are + verb-ing","color":"emerald","text":"This tense is built with the form of be and a verb with -ing.","example":"I am reading. / She is working. / They are waiting."},{"label":"Now or around now","color":"blue","text":"The tense often shows an action at the moment of speaking or in the current period.","example":"We are studying now. / He is staying with friends this week."},{"label":"Temporary situations","color":"amber","text":"Use this tense for a situation that is not permanent.","example":"I am working from home this month."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the form for each subject group.","items":[{"label":"I","title":"am + verb-ing","subtitle":"I am reading. / I'm reading."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"is + verb-ing","subtitle":"He is sleeping. / She's working. / It's raining."},{"label":"You / We / They","title":"are + verb-ing","subtitle":"You are listening. / We're waiting. / They're coming."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"write → writing / sit → sitting","subtitle":"Sometimes the -ing form changes the spelling of the verb."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Present Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Action now","color":"emerald","description":"Show something happening at the moment of speaking.","examples":[{"en":"I am talking to my teacher now.","ua":"I am talking to my teacher now."},{"en":"They are having lunch at the moment.","ua":"They are having lunch at the moment."}]},{"label":"Current period","color":"blue","description":"Use the tense for an action happening this week, today, or these days.","examples":[{"en":"She is reading a new novel this week.","ua":"She is reading a new novel this week."},{"en":"We are learning new grammar this month.","ua":"We are learning new grammar this month."}]},{"label":"Temporary situation","color":"amber","description":"Show something that is not permanent.","examples":[{"en":"He is staying with his aunt this month.","ua":"He is staying with his aunt this month."},{"en":"I am taking the bus these days.","ua":"I am taking the bus these days."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form comparison","intro":"One pattern with be and -ing for all subjects.","rows":[{"en":"I am writing an email.","ua":"am + writing","note":"I + am + verb-ing"},{"en":"She is watching TV.","ua":"is + watching","note":"she + is + verb-ing"},{"en":"We are waiting outside.","ua":"are + waiting","note":"we + are + verb-ing"},{"en":"They are coming home now.","ua":"are + coming","note":"they + are + verb-ing"}],"warning":"Do not say I reading or She is work."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out be before the verb.","wrong":"❌ He working now.","right":"✅ He is working now."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the base form instead of -ing.","wrong":"❌ I am read a book.","right":"✅ I am reading a book."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using the wrong form of be with the subject.","wrong":"❌ They is waiting for us.","right":"✅ They are waiting for us."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Present Continuous = am / is / are + verb-ing.","Use am with I.","Use is with he / she / it.","Use are with you / we / they.","Use this tense for actions happening now or temporarily."]}
